meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Solving Debugging Paralysis: Student Onboarding Guide
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to implement strategic user onboarding patterns using Antigravity and Claude Code to master your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis stems from unclear architectural intent. By implementing a structured user onboarding flow, you force your application to define state transitions explicitly. This clarity allows AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to map your logic, isolate errors, and resolve complex dependencies that previously seemed impossible to debug.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of red error logs. The silence of your IDE is deafening, and that 'simple' feature you started building three days ago has become a labyrinth of broken dependencies. You feel the impostor syndrome creeping in, wondering if you are truly cut out for this.

But here is the secret: every senior engineer has been exactly where you are. The difference between a frustrated student and a ship-ready developer isn't innate intelligence—it's having a system to break down complexity. Today, we turn your debugging nightmare into a masterclass in user onboarding, using AI as your force multiplier to bridge the gap between 'broken code' and 'production ready'.

steps.title: Architecting the Onboarding Flow
steps.description: Define the user journey state machine. Before fixing bugs, map out exactly how a user enters your app. This creates a mental model that makes debugging logic errors significantly easier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Review my current codebase and map out a clean state machine for a user onboarding flow. Identify where the current logic fails to handle edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze current auth flow and generate a state machine diagram in Mermaid.js'

steps.title: Isolating State Dependencies
steps.description: Bugs often hide in shared state variables. Use Antigravity to isolate your onboarding state from the rest of your app logic to simplify the debug surface area.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the onboarding state logic into a separate, testable module. Ensure no side effects impact the main application loop.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file src/onboarding/state.ts --goal 'Encapsulate state and remove global dependencies'

steps.title: Implementing Robust Error Boundaries
steps.description: Instead of crashing, your app should guide users through onboarding gracefully. Implement error boundaries to catch failures at the component level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React Error Boundary component specifically for the onboarding flow that logs errors to a mock service and shows a friendly retry UI.

steps.title: Automating Integration Tests
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write tests that simulate user behavior. If you can automate the onboarding, you can prove the bug is gone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Playwright tests that simulate a user completing the onboarding flow, including an intentional failure scenario to verify my error boundary.

steps.title: Refining the User Feedback Loop
steps.description: Finalize the polish. Ensure that when a user hits a snag, the app provides actionable feedback rather than a cryptic code error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the onboarding UI/UX. Ensure all error states provide clear, non-technical instructions for the end-user to resolve their own issues.

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as 'User Feedback'—if the machine is confused, your user will be too.
Always build your onboarding flow as a finite state machine; it makes debugging a logic puzzle rather than a guessing game.
Use Claude Code to document your 'Why' in the code comments, not just the 'How'—this is what separates juniors from seniors.
If you get stuck for more than 20 minutes, delete the function and rewrite it with an AI pair programmer; don't sink costs into bad code.
Version control your prompts. Keep a text file of your successful Antigravity prompts to reuse for future features.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, integrate a data persistence layer to save user progress. This will teach you how to handle database migrations and async state synchronization.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn any debugging block into a solved feature. The only thing missing is consistent action. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
